posts.text:
I want to know! Did you use AI during the L.A. wildfires?

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/d…

Columbia Journalism Review (@CJR)

The LA Fires and what a disaster says about our physical and informational environments, by @Jon_Allsop. cjr.org/the_media_today/la_w…




— https://nitter.net/CJR/status/1878829345621659711#m

Matt Pearce 🦅🇺🇸 (@mattdpearce)

TAX ZUCKERBERG, FUND KICK-ASS LOCAL JOURNALISTS 

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/c…



— https://nitter.net/mattdpearce/status/1878607938547585212#m

TAX ZUCKERBERG, FUND KICK-ASS LOCAL JOURNALISTS 

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/c…

“The humans get up onstage and start thanking everybody, but most typically, in statistical descending order, 1. Their family, 2. God, 3. their agent, and 4. Steven Spielberg”

Since most of my winner picks are probably wrong I’m going to see if these stats stand tonight

Happy Oscars night:

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/exit-holly...
ok but can we talk about the mystery millionaire of osaka giving the city a bunch of gold to maintain the sewer system

we know our zoomer war videos are terrible but if you hate them you hate America 

www.wsj.com/politics/pol...
FCC Chair Brendan Carr threatens broadcast licenses over Iran War coverage
“But, as you see, it's a beautiful day, the Strait of Hormuz is open and people are having a wonderful time.”

NEW: Our team of FT reporters across the world dug into the strike on the school in Minab. Along the way, we explored what the dearth of real-time, on-the-ground information means for open-source research in this conflict:

as.ft.com/r/f29c784b-3...
The Strait of Hormuz is open for transit
This is the best lede I've seen in a tech story in...years? There should be an award just for ledes.
there's a guy named "fulton rutland" competing in bull riding rn
WBAL in Baltimore helping viewers understand the difference between watches and warnings
lmao incredible new euphemism unlocked: I’m on a flight and they just said we’re going to be going through an area of “enhanced” turbulence
paging Clausewitz bluesky

this junkballing Czech electrician is a folk hero in Japan for striking out Ohtani in the last WBC (stopped for photos/autographs etc) but he's retiring after this bc Czech league ball doesn't pay the bills.
I love the way he took in in this one last surreal moment before going back to normalcy
Continuous scroll (aka endless scroll) and autoplay next videos are some of the most devastating technologies ever invented.
Thank you for letting me distract you long enough to read it!
Just came across this post by Matt Pearce. I really appreciate it — and — need to work on curbing my social media addiction [I say on social media]
I’m there with you… I love the garage door metaphor. Open to the world, but not for business.
